name: Summit Funding, Inc.
description_short: Summit Funding, Inc. is a residential mortgage lender based in Chicago specializing in home purchase, refinance, and mortgage services with a focus on personalized customer support.
description_long:
Summit Funding, Inc. operates as a residential mortgage lender with a Chicago branch located at 325 W Huron St, Ste 506. The company has positioned itself as a specialized lender focused exclusively on residential mortgage lending, distinguishing itself from larger diversified financial institutions by narrowing their service offering to one core product category.

The company offers residential mortgage services including home purchase loans and refinancing options. Customers can work with one of six identified loan professionals on staff, including branch managers Tom Merritt and Michael Black, along with loan officers Michael Dufour, Teresa Giannini, Ryan Jewett, and Todd Merritt. All loan officers carry individual NMLS identifications, and the branch itself holds NMLS ID# 1140792, indicating regulatory registration and oversight.

Summit Funding emphasizes relationship-building and customer-first service as their competitive differentiator. Their messaging focuses on creating a "better, more supportive experience" for homebuyers and emphasizes that "every one of our home loan professionals is committed to delivering exceptional mortgage services, building lasting relationships, and achieving meaningful results." The company positions homeownership as a pathway to building better futures.

Based on available data, Summit Funding maintains a 4.92 rating across 1,055 total reviews on their profile. Customer feedback highlights responsiveness, knowledgeable staff, ease of working with the team, and fast communication. However, the company's online presence and marketing materials appear limited to their Chicago branch location, suggesting potential geographic constraints or limited multi-state operations visibility.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: High customer rating of 4.92 stars with 1,055 documented reviews demonstrating consistent performance
Specialized focus exclusively on residential lending with no competing product lines
Multiple experienced loan officers and branch managers available for personalized service
Customers specifically cite responsive communication and knowledgeable staff in reviews
Regulated NMLS entities with individual loan officer NMLS identifications for transparency
Both purchase and refinance loan options available through single provider
Emphasis on relationship-building and customer-first approach documented in company mission
cons: Limited geographic presence visible—only Chicago branch information provided on website, suggesting restricted service area
No transparent pricing, rate information, or loan product details disclosed on public-facing website
No information about loan types offered (FHA, VA, conventional, jumbo, etc.) or lending criteria
Minimal digital functionality shown—website requires selecting a specific loan officer to apply rather than streamlined online process
No details on APR ranges, fees, closing costs, or competitive rate comparison data available
best_for: Chicago-area homebuyers seeking personalized service from experienced mortgage professionals
Homeowners refinancing existing mortgages who value relationship-based lending approach
Borrowers who prioritize direct communication and responsiveness over digital-first lending platforms
First-time homebuyers wanting guidance from established loan officers with individual NMLS credentials
diagnosis: Summit Funding is best suited for Chicago-area borrowers who value personalized service and direct relationships with experienced loan officers over self-service digital platforms. The main caveat is the lack of public transparency regarding rates, fees, loan product specifics, and geographic service limitations beyond Chicago, requiring borrowers to contact the branch directly for pricing and eligibility details.
services: Online mortgage application (through loan officer selection)
In-person branch services at Chicago location
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 900 N Franklin St Suite 401, 503, and 505, Chicago, IL 60610
